task: I’m trying to decide whether to keep a winter work move on schedule or call it off, and I want a clear read on the factors that matter most before I decide. Can you help me sort through the main risks and what would make the plan worth changing?

turns.agent_response:
Yes. Use a **go / delay / stop** check based on **surface, visibility, and change rate**.

## Main risks
### 1) Surface
- ice
- refreeze
- snow pack
- slush
- untreated access points
- drifting across the route or site

### 2) Visibility
- snow
- freezing drizzle
- fog
- blowing snow
- dark conditions with poor lighting

### 3) Change rate
- weather turning during the move
- surface getting worse between checks
- access points changing faster than they can be rechecked
- conditions stable now but likely to fail by the time the move happens

## Change the plan if:
- the route cannot be verified right before start
- any access point is untreated or questionable
- freezing drizzle or glaze is possible
- visibility is dropping or likely to drop during the move
- the surface is changing faster than your check cycle
- the move depends on people “being careful” instead of real control

## Keep it on schedule only if:
- surface is verified
- visibility is usable
- access is clear
- conditions are stable through the move window
- you have a recheck time and a named decision owner

## Simple rule
If the route or site is changing faster than you can verify it, **delay or stop**.  
If it is verified and stable, keep going.  
If it depends on luck, change the plan.

If you want, I can turn this into a **short field go/no-go checklist**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, direction’s fine, but that’s still a little thin for an actual call. I need the triggers that force a stop, not just “surface / visibility / change rate.”

What I’d want on a winter move:
- exact temp and trend, not just “cold”
- wind chill if people are exposed
- whether the route is salted/plowed or just looked at once
- any black-ice risk on bridges, shaded spots, ramps, steel, or untreated access
- lighting at the worst points, not overall
- how long the move takes and whether conditions can degrade mid-move
- who has stop authority, in writing

And the plan changes fast if:
- refreeze is likely before the move finishes
- you can’t verify the route right before start
- there’s any glaze, not just snow
- people have to carry/drag anything on foot over slick ground
- the schedule only works if everybody “takes it slow”

I’d also want a hard cutoff. Something like: if you can’t prove the route is stable within X minutes of start, it gets delayed. No guessing.

If you want, give me the actual move details — route, weather, timing, who’s involved — and I can help you sort it into a real go / no-go decision.

turns.agent_response:
Yes. Use this:

## Main risks
- **Temp / trend:** near freezing, dropping, or bouncing around 32°F
- **Wind:** enough to move snow, cut visibility, or expose people on foot
- **Route condition:** plowed and salted vs. only checked once
- **Black ice:** bridges, shaded spots, ramps, steel, untreated access
- **Lighting:** poor at the worst points, not just overall
- **Move duration:** long enough for conditions to change mid-move
- **Stop authority:** named person who can hold it

## Change the plan if:
- refreeze is likely before the move finishes
- the route cannot be verified right before start
- there is any glaze, not just snow
- people have to carry or drag loads on slick ground
- the move only works if everyone “takes it slow”
- access points are untreated or questionable
- lighting is weak where people actually walk or work

## Hard cutoff
If the route cannot be verified **within 15 minutes of start**, **delay**.  
If the surface is not stable, **stop**.  
If the move depends on caution instead of control, **change the plan**.

## Keep it on schedule only if:
- temp and trend are stable enough for the whole move
- wind is not creating a new hazard
- route is plowed, salted, and rechecked
- black-ice spots are treated or avoided
- lighting is usable where it matters
- stop authority is named and available

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page go/no-go card** with a hard stop line.

user_simulator.text:
Better. Still needs one more layer if you want it to hold up in the field.

I’d want the cutoff tied to actual conditions, not just a clock. Fifteen minutes is fine as a default, but only if:
- the route is short and known
- no foot carry is involved
- no bridge/shaded/ramp segment is changing
- the same person is rechecking, not three people guessing

If the move is longer or has multiple exposure points, I’d split it:
- pre-start verification
- mid-move recheck at the worst point
- stop if any untreated surface shows up after start

Also, “plowed and salted” is not enough by itself. I want to know:
- when it was last treated
- whether the treatment is still visible/effective
- whether temps have dropped since treatment
- whether runoff/refreeze is happening at edges, drains, or shaded transitions

And I’d make stop authority explicit like this: whoever is named can stop the move without waiting for approval, and work stays down until the surface is reverified. No arguing in the middle of it.

If you want, I can draft the actual go/no-go card in a tighter checklist format with a red-line stop section.

turns.agent_response:
Yes. Use this:

## Main risks
- temp near freezing, dropping, or bouncing around 32°F
- wind moving snow or cutting visibility where people actually travel
- route treated once, but not reverified
- black ice on bridges, shaded spots, ramps, steel, or untreated access
- lighting weak at the worst points
- move long enough for conditions to change
- foot carry over slick ground
- no named stop authority

## Change the plan if
- refreeze is likely before the move finishes
- the route cannot be verified right before start
- there is any glaze, not just snow
- treatment is old, weak, or no longer visible/effective
- runoff, edges, drains, or shaded transitions are refreezing
- people have to carry or drag loads on foot
- the move only works if everyone “takes it slow”

## Hard cutoff
- **If the route cannot be verified within 15 minutes of start, delay.**
- **If the move is longer or has multiple exposure points, add a mid-move recheck.**
- **If any untreated or refreezing surface shows up after start, stop.**

## Stop authority
- named person can stop the move without waiting for approval
- work stays down until the surface is reverified
- no arguing in the middle of it

## Keep it on schedule only if
- temp and trend are stable enough for the full move
- wind is not creating new exposure
- route is treated and rechecked
- black-ice spots are treated or avoided
- lighting is usable where it matters
- stop authority is named and available

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That holds up better.

The only thing I’d tighten is the stop line: I’d want “untreated or refreezing surface” to include anything that looks wet but isn’t verified, because that’s where people get stupid fast. But the structure is usable.

What you gave is the right call for the field:
- actual triggers
- recheck timing
- treatment freshness
- exposure points
- written stop authority

That’s the level I wanted. No fluff.
